title: Test portable update flow (Windows + Linux) with a version bump
description: Windows portable artifact is validated (fresh install, model download, generation all OK). Next: validate the UPDATE path on both Windows and Linux. Requires a version bump (e.g. 0.0.1 -> 0.0.2) so there is a newer release to update to. Steps: (1) bump APP_VERSION; (2) build a full portable + matching update bundle for each platform; (3) Windows: install the older version, run update.bat (online, GitHub release) AND update-from-zip.bat (offline, local zip) and confirm engine/models/user-data/projects are preserved and the app launches on the new version; (4) Linux: repeat with update.sh / update-from-zip.sh; (5) confirm the no-terminal default launchers (start.vbs / start.sh) still work post-update. Update-bundle staging + update-manifest already exist in scripts/build-portable.mjs; this card is about exercising them end-to-end. Note: online update.bat/update.sh pull the latest GitHub Release asset, so a real (or pre-release) release tag is needed to test the online path; update-from-zip can be tested offline immediately. Absolute path of master repo: c:\AI\Mpi\Cubric-Vision
